examples.text:
ويقولون للّذي يُحْمَلُ عليه الميّت النَّعَاشُ. والصّواب النَّعْشُ. قال الشّاعر:
أَمَحْمُولٌ عَلَى النَّعْشِ الهُمَامُ
examples.english:
Them man say for that on which one is carried dead naʕāš. The right is naʕš. A poet (al-Nābiḡa aḏ-Ḏubyāni) said:
I am carried high on the bier.

senses.gloss: to animate, to revive, to restore in health or wealth
to say to someone that he shall rise

etymology.text: Seemingly a Middle Iranian borrowing equivalent to Persian لاش (lâš). Prefixation to ع ي ش (ʕ y š) as in نَوْرَج (nawraj) is also considered. The verbs in the root ن ع ش (n ʕ š) are denominal in any case.
